title: sedum rupestre subsp. forsterianum
intro: Sedum rupestre subsp. forsterianum, commonly known as Forster's stonecrop, is a succulent plant species belonging to the Crassulaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating skin conditions and wounds, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Forster's stonecrop include its ability to soothe and calm irritated skin, promote wound healing, and exhibit antioxidant and anti-aging effects. Herbal preparations of Sedum rupestre subsp. forsterianum typically involve infusions or tinctures made from the plant's leaves, stems, or roots, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
